title: Customs Clearance I — 12 Main Tests
description: 12 topic-based tests covering all chapters of Customs Clearance I.

tests.title: Test 1: Prohibited and Restricted Goods
tests.description: 50-question assessment on Prohibited and Restricted Goods. Based on EACCMA 2004 and Customs Clearance I notes.

questions.question: Prohibited goods are best defined as goods that:
options: are not allowed to be imported, exported, or transferred
can only be imported through airports

questions.question: Restricted goods are goods whose importation or exportation is:
options: allowed only after meeting specific conditions

questions.question: Under EACCMA, prohibited goods include goods whose importation, exportation, or carriage coastwise is:
options: prohibited under the Act or any law in force

questions.question: Which of the following best distinguishes prohibited goods from restricted goods?
options: Prohibited goods are taxed more heavily
Prohibited goods are not allowed at all, while restricted goods may be allowed subject to conditions

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a prohibited good?
Which of the following is listed as a prohibited item in the notes?
Matches made using which substance are prohibited?
Narcotic drugs under international control are treated as:
Hazardous wastes and their disposal are prohibited mainly for:
Which of the following is a restricted good?
Arms and ammunition specified under Chapter 93 are classified in the notes as:
Worked ivory and articles of ivory are:
Which of the following is listed as a restricted good?
Endangered species of world flora and fauna and their products are restricted in accordance with:
Commercial casings mentioned in the notes refer to:
Unwrought precious metals and precious stones are treated as:
Which of the following is a reason for restricting goods?
options: To control importation or exportation through specific conditions

questions.question: Restricted goods are usually controlled by:
options: specific government agencies depending on their nature

questions.question: Goods specified in Part A of the Third Schedule are:
Goods specified in Part B of the Third Schedule are:
According to the notes, clearance for restricted goods shall only be granted against:
options: permits, certificates, or other authority from a relevant agency

questions.question: Which of the following is listed among restricted exports?
options: Fresh unprocessed Nile perch and tilapia

questions.question: Which of the following is listed among prohibited exports?
options: Used or scrap cables and conductors of aluminium, steel, and copper

questions.question: Waste and scrap of ferrous cast iron are classified in the notes as:
Timber from wood grown in the Partner States is listed as:
Wood charcoal is classified under the notes as:
Used automobile batteries, lead scrap, crude and refined lead, and all forms of scrap metal are:
Warehoused goods, goods under duty drawback, and transshipped goods should not be exported in vessels of less than:
Which of the following is NOT one of the reasons stated in the notes for prohibiting or restricting goods?
Which of the following is one of the stated reasons for prohibition or restriction?
Which of the following is one of the stated reasons for prohibition or restriction?
Which of the following is one of the stated reasons for prohibition or restriction?
If a good is restricted, this means:
options: it is banned in all circumstances
it may be cleared if the required conditions are fulfilled

questions.question: If a good is prohibited, this means:
options: it may enter after payment of extra duty
it may be imported with an invoice only
it is not allowed under the law
it must first go to a transit shed

questions.question: Which of the following goods is associated mainly with biodiversity and conservation control?
Which of the following goods is most directly linked to biosafety regulation?
Which of the following is most clearly a security-related restricted item?
Which of the following is most clearly a health-related prohibited item?
options: Distilled beverages containing injurious chemical products

questions.question: Which statement is correct?
options: Restricted goods may be allowed if conditions are met
Prohibited goods may be cleared by paying warehouse rent

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a good that would typically require permit-based clearance?
A shipment of endangered animal products arrives without the required authority. Under the notes, the goods should be treated as:
options: restricted goods lacking the necessary clearance conditions

questions.question: A person attempts to export scrap copper conductors. According to the notes, these are:
Fresh unprocessed fish such as Nile perch and tilapia are listed as:
Traps capable of killing or capturing game animals are:
options: restricted and require a written permit

questions.question: Postal franking machines are:
options: restricted goods that require a written permit

questions.question: Bone, tortoise shell, horn, antlers, coral, and mother-of-pearl articles are treated as:
Which of the following best describes how prohibited imports should generally be handled?
options: Cleared after payment of double duty
Not allowed and subject to enforcement action

questions.question: Which of the following best describes how restricted imports should generally be handled?
options: They may be cleared once the required approvals are obtained
They are never examined by Customs

questions.question: Which pair is correctly matched?
Which statement best reflects the overall purpose of controlling prohibited and restricted goods?
options: To ensure goods move without any checks
To protect society, revenue, security, health, and the environment through legal control of trade
To replace all partner government agencies
